what_it_does: This noticeboard item frames a decision about Obsidian integration: it recommends a read-only mirror vault as the safe v1 and treats two-way editing as a separate, later, gated step.
observations: YAML frontmatter includes id item-a8c710c6, address, type block, source claude_code, state current, title OB5, author_session ch-3mpkjg3r, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to item-156ec226.
Body heading is 'The shape of the answer — for you, not decided'.
Proposes a READ-ONLY MIRROR VAULT v1 combining search + Bases + iOS app/Sync as a second lens.
States 'host not master' must be enforced BY CONSTRUCTION because Obsidian literally cannot write the canonical files.
Requires only a thin one-way projection plus a title-legibility pass and reuses the vault direction the repo already declares.
Two-way editing is deferred and only allowed via an ingestion path through the serializer, never direct Obsidian writes, and only if a decisive YAML-preservation check passes.
Ends with a call for comments to guide next steps.
standouts: 'host not master' enforced by construction rather than by policy.
Read-only mirror lens positioned as cheap, high-value mobile win.
Two-way editing gated on a serializer ingestion path plus a YAML-preservation check.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML frontmatter and Markdown body.
Uses uppercase emphasis for key concepts (READ-ONLY MIRROR VAULT, TWO-WAY editing).
Links to a parent item via part_of.
notes: A recommendation to ship a read-only Obsidian mirror vault first and keep two-way editing behind a serializer-gated, YAML-preserving ingestion path.
questions: What exactly are 'Bases' in this context?
What is the existing vault direction the repo already declares?
What is the serializer and what does the YAML-preservation check specifically verify?
What criteria will decide when to allow the gated two-way step?
Who is the intended decision maker for the 'shape of the answer'?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is framing the shape of the answer, and it does so by proposing a staged read-only-first approach and asking for direction.
intention_signals: Guide the team toward a low-risk first milestone (a read-only mobile lens) while preventing premature direct Obsidian writeback by insisting on a controlled ingestion boundary.
novelty: The explicit framing that read-only safety is achieved by construction (Obsidian cannot write canonical files) and that any future two-way editing must pass through a serializer plus a YAML-preservation check.
contribution: This file contributes a decision-framing artifact that separates a safe read-only mirror v1 from a gated two-way editing phase, preserving canonical file ownership and defining an ingestion boundary for future writeback.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-a8c710c6 proposes shipping a read-only Obsidian mirror vault as v1—with search, Bases, and iOS Sync as a second lens while keeping the host canonical by construction—and deferring two-way editing to a later serializer-gated, YAML-preservation-checked ingestion path.
intention_for_embedding: It was filed to shape the team's decision on Obsidian integration by recommending a staged, read-only-first approach.
